articles.title: Delhi Weather: Thunderstorm Activity Likely To Hit Parts Of City Today Evening; Will Heatwave Relief End Soon? - The Daily Jagran
articles.full_text:
Delhi Weather: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast mostly clear skies across Delhi-NCR on Thursday. The weather body has however warned of thunderstorms during the afternoon and evening hours in multiple areas.
Thunderstorms could hit North Delhi, North-East Delhi, North-West Delhi, West Delhi, South Delhi, South-West Delhi, South-East Delhi, New Delhi, and Shahdara during the day. Meanwhile, NCR locations like Noida, Ghaziabad and Gurugram will witness similar conditions during the evening, keeping the temperatures low.
Heatwave Relief To End Soon
This relief is likely to be short-lived as IMD has warned that temperatures are set to rise, reaching near 40 degrees Celsius in the week. The rest of the week and days after that are expected to remain dry.
Punjab Weather
A yellow weather alert has been issued for Punjab on Thursday. The weather will change again on Sunday, and the yellow alert will remain in effect for three days. Dust storms are likely during this time.

Haryana Weather
Weather in Haryana is expected to remain clear until May 10, following which a new western disturbance will become active. The IMD has issued a rain warning in this regard. The recent continuous rainfall has led to a drop in night temperatures, with the minimum temperature in Rohtak reaching 15.6 degrees. On Wednesday, Hisar and surrounding districts remained sunny.
